ddls:
CREATE TABLE "variants" (
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each variant */,
  "variant_name" TEXT /* Name of the variant (e.g., Omicron, Alpha, Beta) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the variant */,
  "mutation_type" TEXT /* Type of mutation (e.g., point mutation, deletion) */,
  "transmissibility" REAL /* Transmissibility of the variant (0-1 scale) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the variant (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  "vaccine_effectiveness" REAL /* Effectiveness of vaccines against the variant (0-1 scale) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variant_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case record */,
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variant associated with the case */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the case was reported */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the case was reported */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of cases for the variant on the given date */,
  "total" INTEGER /* Total number of cases for the variant */,
  "percent_in_NYC" REAL /* Percentage of cases in NYC */,
  "hospitalization_rate" REAL /* Hospitalization rate for the variant */,
  "case_fatality_rate" REAL /* Case fatality rate for the variant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_cases_date FOREIGN KEY ("date") REFERENCES dates ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_cases_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_cases_variant_id FOREIGN KEY ("variant_id") REFERENCES variants ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dates" (
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each date */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the case data was reported */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week (e.g., Monday, Tuesday) */,
  "week_of_year" INTEGER /* Week of the year (1-52) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., NYC, Other) */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., city, state) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variant_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the type (e.g., Alpha, Beta, Omicron) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold record */,
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variant associated with the threshold */,
  "threshold_value" INTEGER /* Threshold value for the variant (e.g., 100, 500) */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "notification_recipients" TEXT /* Notification recipients (e.g., researchers, healthcare professionals) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_thresholds_variant_id FOREIGN KEY ("variant_id") REFERENCES variants ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variant associated with the alert */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the alert was triggered */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message associated with the alert */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Notification status (e.g., sent, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_variant_id FOREIGN KEY ("variant_id") REFERENCES variants ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, healthcare professional, admin) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location associated with the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variant being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the variant */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the variant was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "data_downloaded" INTEGER /* Whether data was downloaded (true/false) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_variant_id FOREIGN KEY ("variant_id") REFERENCES variants ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, healthcare professional, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variant_sequencing" (
  "sequencing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequencing record */,
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variant associated with the sequencing data */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample used for sequencing */,
  "sequencing_date" TEXT /* Date the sequencing was performed */,
  "sequence_data" TEXT /* Sequencing data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequencing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_sequencing_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_sequencing_variant_id FOREIGN KEY ("variant_id") REFERENCES variants ("variant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "sample_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the sample was collected */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the sample was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geographic_data" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)
